title: Unclear default value for --userns
body:
The manual page of podman-run says about `--userns`: “Set the user namespace mode for the container. It defaults to the `PODMAN_USERNS` environment variable.”  I have no PODMAN_USERNS environment variable.

What is the default value for `--userns` (in rootless and rootful mode)?

Podamn Version:      4.3.0-dev

comments.body:
```
Set the user namespace mode for the container. It defaults to the **PODMAN_USERNS** environment variable.
An empty value ("") means user namespaces are disabled unless an explicit mapping is set with the
**--uidmap** and **--gidmap** options.
```

Since the `PODMAN_USERNS` when unset it is equal to `""`, the default value is to not use user namespaces, so the equivalent of `--userns host`

Do you think we need this in a man page.  We should change the wording to say that the container runs in the default userns in rootless mode.  Not that there is no user namespace.

--userns and --uidap --gidmap will put the container into an alternative user namespace then the default.

In rootful mode the user namespace is the default user namespace.
In rootless mode the user namespace is the current UID mapped to root and then the contents or /etc/subuid (/etc/subgid) for the user running podman starting at UID=1.
For me an unset variable and a variable with value emtpy string are different things in the general case.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
